z'Return the Euler totient function of n.
{Function is unused by library code. If you use this code, please open an issue in https://github.com/tlsfuzzer/python-ecdsar+
phi~
Return Carmichael function of n.
    Carmichael(n) is the smallest integer x such that
    m**x = 1 mod n for all m relatively prime to n.
    rS
carmichael_of_factorizedrR
carmichael
zlReturn the Carmichael function of a number that is
    represented as a list of (prime,exponent) pairs.
    rS
carmichael_of_ppowerr 
f_listrO
z:Carmichael function of the given power of the given prime.rS
ppr"
z8Return the order of x in the multiplicative group mod m.rS
	order_mod
r qD|
z5Return the largest factor of a relatively prime to b.rS
largest_factor_relatively_prime
z}Return the order of x in the multiplicative group mod m',
    where m' is the largest factor of m relatively prime to x.
    rS
kinda_order_mod
}	|	d
}	|	d
Return True if x is prime, False otherwise.
    We use the Miller-Rabin test, as given in Menezes et al. p. 138.
    This test is not exact: there are composite values n for which
    it returns True.
    In testing the odd numbers from 10000001 to 19999999,
    about 66 composites got past the first test,
    5 got past the second test, and none got past the third.
    Since factors of 2, 3, 5, 7, and 11 were detected during
    preliminary screening, the number of numbers tested by
    Miller-Rabin was (19999999 - 10000001)*(2/3)*(4/5)*(6/7)
    = 4.57 million.
    r
miller_rabin_test_countrK
math
logr 
n_bitsr(
ttr)
z9Return the smallest prime larger than the starting value.r
starting_valuerO
next_prime^

Z	six.movesr
